hints.title: Nudge
hints.content: Think about how the login form might be checking credentials. What happens if the query logic can be altered?

hints.title: Direction
hints.content: The login query likely looks like: SELECT * FROM users WHERE username='...' AND password='...'. What if you could make the WHERE clause always true?

hints.title: Approach
hints.content: Try injecting a single quote into the username field to break out of the string. Then use an OR condition that always evaluates to true.

hints.title: Near-solution
hints.content: Use the username: ' OR 1=1 -- and any password. The -- comments out the rest of the query.

hints.title: Solution
hints.content: Enter ' OR 1=1 -- as the username and anything as the password. This modifies the SQL query to SELECT * FROM users WHERE username='' OR 1=1 --' AND password='...', which returns all users and logs you in as the first user (admin).
